I have cooked my kids white lasagna, regular lasagna, Italian subs, Cesar salad wraps, etc. I learned from him seasoning in layers, doing a bechamel sauce, enchiladas. I love this man!!!!

body: HE DESERVES THIS SO BAD. As a young cancer survivor, I love seeing him thrive. I wanted to go to culinary school, but it was too physically taxing on my body. Never gave up the love for cooking though. I screamed when I saw this in my feed ❤.

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: My dad also had cancer about the same time that Kevin did. Since my dad was stuck for hours doing chemo or fluid draining, plus he had rough days where he couldn't be active, we spent a lot of time sending each other short and fun video clips. Kevmo was one of my dad's favorites - not because of the shared cancer experience, but just because he was chill, making good hands-on food and had an epic beard. Sadly, my dad didn't make it, but I fondly remember him whenever I watch Kevin's videos. Food brings humans together. Live to eat, don't just eat to live.
author: @clunkclunk2099

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking

title: Students discuss their paths to remedial classes
body:
What people need to understand is that it's a combination of the how the instructor teaches their ciriculum and how the student intakes the information.

In high school, we had one of the hardest Algebra 1 text that took the principles and exponentially made the material harder than it should be. And most of it consisted of word problems which I has trouble translating. Hence I got an F.

In the community college system, there was more hand-held holding and the use of online computer homework made it easier. Hence, I actually got A's in elementary and intermediate algebra. Additionally, on the quarter system, there is even less time to cover every single topic in a 10 week course. Additionally, there were only a couple word problems on the test, which made it easier. Additionally, instructors would have a day for review of what types of problems that could be on the exam or final.

Yes, the first time I took intermediate algebra after high school I did fail it. But I got the equivalents of an A when I took the class a decade later. Do I remember most of it nope?

But sin^2theta + cos^2theta=1 was mailed into my head. But that was from precalculus. I took that class 3 timess. But some students never make it to precalculus. And if they do, trig us covered so fast, they might not get it.

If I took calculus 1, yes. I would review everything.

However, I must say to all you commenter that it's not always the students fault and some college instructors teach intermediate algebra from a calculus or physycs perspective and thus never communicate the simple principles of algebra.

For example, there is an instructor at my college who has been there for years, but there have been so many complaints about how bad his methodology is. You shouldn't teach intermediate algebra from a physics or stem point of view. Teach it at its most basic of basics rise/run. And if there is a question regarding ball trajectory in a parabolic fashion, teachers need to go over how the quadratic equation applies on regards to height or just the first part of the equation x= -b/2a and then plug that in to the original quadratic equation which is usually -16t^2 + initial velocity T + h (which =0 of you throw it up).

This is a traditional intermediate algebra test that is SIMPLE if the teacher goes over it in class.

Now, a hard teacher would want 75% to get it wrong, so he or she would add in that it bounced 3 additional times. That's not necessary for intermediate remedial algebra. It's not physycs. It's algebra. Teach the basics so students can pass.

That is my point. It's about HOW you teach it and the resources given to you. If you've never taken physics how can your teacher expect you to apply to the equation that the ball bounces 3 additional times like a mechanics 1 class would write? They should not exist that out of algebra students.

It really can be simple. But since teachers make it so complicated instead of etching the most basics of basics, they are going to loose half their class in lala land.
author: @DerekMckean

title: Students discuss their paths to remedial classes
body: I actually agree with remedial classes. Especially for older students like myself that are returning to college. I'm going to be taking math 95 or 96 at csn. Even though I grasp more advanced math, I tend to test horribly on the Accuplacer and would need to take the refresher course. The Accuplacer exam is very difficult.

He's at UNLV taking a remedial class.
To get accepted u need a

U probably got the 21 or higher on act test. But scored really low on math section u need at least a 22.
Thats a college requirement. That's why u need a remedial course but who knows 🤷

title: I Stayed at 21 Las Vegas Hotels in 21 Days – Here's What You Need To Know
body:
We have stayed at the Bellagio, Caesar’s, Palazzo, Treasure Island, Mirage, Venetian and Wynn. We have been inside of every property on the strip. We preferred the Venetian overall for the price. Largest rooms, Grand Canal Shops, best spa/gym on the strip, and not crazy expensive. If you wanna spend up, the Wynn is our favorite higher end. For budget, I would stay at TI.

Always do the $20 sandwich at check in. Location is our #1 priority and we don’t gamble much.
author: @stlbigbad23

title: I Stayed at 21 Las Vegas Hotels in 21 Days – Here's What You Need To Know
body: As a local, this was fascinating to watch! Seeing the rapid-fire contrast of 21 properties in 21 days is brilliant. It gives such a unique, on-the-ground perspective on how our city's hospitality truly scales. Cheers!
author: @TMslowliving

title: I Stayed at 21 Las Vegas Hotels in 21 Days – Here's What You Need To Know
body:
I stayed at the Luxor about two months ago for a whole week. My room was the exact neighboring room to the room shown in the video. It is very big but everything is old and looks used, the cleanliness is ok but not perfect. It was the cheapest on the strip and you get what you pay for. My biggest concern was the elevators, they run at an angle and jerk as if they are about to plunge into the depths and there are loose pieces of paneling hanging on the inside wall. In general this hotel is very much in need of renovation, there were two buckets in our corridor catching drops from the ceiling (for the whole week). The pool is large but most of it is permanently closed so it gets very crowded. Also there is absolutely no shade by the pool, you are forced to lie in the blazing sun or rent a tent.
The inside of the hotel is very cool for a first time in Vegas, it's a complete sensory overload, colorful lights everywhere, loud noises, music and people laughing. The Mexican restaurant Diablos Cantina is highly recommended.
By the way, if you pay for parking at the Luxor ($18/day), you can use your room key to park in all the parking garages of the hotels belonging to MGM, including the Bellagio. This is a better starting point for exploring the Strip on foot.
I loved Vegas but would prefer a hotel in the Arts District next time, Vegas is much more than just the Strip!
author: @finn989

title: I Stayed at 21 Las Vegas Hotels in 21 Days – Here's What You Need To Know

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was in psych lock down 2 weeks ago:  no phone or computer, no fresh air, no sunshine, no therapy offered, I felt like I came out worse than I went in, and I was suicidal.  Things MUST change.  Our society is failing us.
author: @zengwenyoga2894

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: To Rob…I remember first hand the feeling of visiting my wife in the psych ward and then going home to two young kids. In a couple of weeks, we celebrate 48 years. Hang tough, man. She’s an impressive lady.
author: @rcp2212

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: You're very brave to put yourself out there when you're in such a vulnerable position. Be proud of that, not many people could do it.
author: @ronr6450

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: It is shocking how different psych wards around the world are. I'm in Germany and here you are not supposed to be all alone in your room. You get a routine to follow. And this routine contains the gym, group therapy, single therapy, arts, meditation and other activities. There is a lot of talking between patients and the staff. Its like a controlled holiday.
author: @larakorrat

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I have been admitted 3 times to a phych ward  some of the nicest most supportive people i ever met were patients
author: @MissMadiGD

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: How difficult and painful it is to live with mental disorders. Those who do not have them will never understand what hell it is.
author: @purpur7187

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Im Nurse who worked on Psychic Ward for year s. You see so many cases from Drug Addiction, Mental illness so much more. God BLESS your Journey
author: @melanieeggers9667

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: She's so full of life and very articulate. Schizophrenia is such a cruel disease. I wish her nothing but the best.😊
author: @mike4619

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I've been in a psych ward over ten times during my 39 years of life. I'm haven't been in a psych ward since April of 2007. Which is awesome and I'm so proud of myself for that.
author: @Bubbles1984

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was held for 5 days involuntarily after visiting the ER for panic attacks and horrible anxiety I've battled my whole life. Never once saw a doctor and never recieved a single medication including the ones I was prescribed. No clothing, no phones, no outdoor time, no visitors, and the worst part was I just had oral surgery and had lost all my teeth due to a bad infection in my jaw bone which needed a bone graft so I was unable to eat because they had no accommodation for that diet. Let's just say that's a birthday I will never forget. When asking for meds, the nurse pulled my file and there was an intake sheet with a picture of a 14 year old female in it ( I am a 32 year old male). They couldnt even handle the correct documents to assist in any sort of help.  Still to this day I have ptsd due to my stay.
author: @TheOneTheOnlyKJ

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
My mom was in psych wards multiple times. The last time, I warned the nurses that she was lying and just putting on a face of someone that was “fine” and was there for no reason. I told them to not listen to anything she was saying and the nurses said that they would tell the doctors. She was out and back home two day later.. Exactly ONE week later, she committed suicide!
My mom was always the person that everyone wanted to be around. She was beautiful, kind, intelligent, and the life of the party. NO ONE expected her to do what she did EXCEPT her kids. My brothers and I seen this side of her every day. It was very hard listening to the side that everyone else got because she had been saying for five years that this was what she was going to do😢
Miss you mom❤
author: @squirrelsquad3.0

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
I am in a YouTube rabbit hole right now. I do not experience anything remotely like what you are living with. I admire the hell out of how you have chosen to beautify and help our world. You are a strong and courageous woman.
Thank you.
author: @Razedot

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I've been committed about 15 times. I've finally found a balance with therapy and medication. It's been hell, but I keep chugging along.
author: @mr.bipolar3344

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This is destroying me! For the last 3 years my 62 year old husband of 35 years had a psychotic break. It reached a point where he is committed by the state. He was starving himself. I know he’s sitting there everyday, just sitting. Keep running little girl, keep fighting, the people who love you need you to fight.
author: @Radar2727

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
this is a freaking 5 star hotel.....the place I went...you can not go outside.... cant have phones, cant talk to the other sex, also we could not even be alone any where....wow. I know this is a year old, but you are a strong person and a beautiful soul.

Edit* Thank you for all the likes.
author: @dalekkgames5430

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Spent 40+ years in and out of psychiatric hospitals until we discovered I had a biological problem, not a psychological one. Changed my diet and changed my life. Been mentally free for over 10 years now.
author: @66sprint

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
When I was in my late 20s I admitted myself into a psych hospital for suicidal thoughts and because I was experiencing things in my life that I did not know how to handle.
At the time, the hospital was sort of a safe haven for me. At the time it’s what I needed for support of what I was going through.
Sometimes people just need that hospital for things they’re going through.
Wishing you the best.
author: @crystalclear6660

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I'm an american, but I live in the UK. I was in the psyc ward for 3 months in full-blown psychosis. They were really good to me & the patients were great. I didn't eat for the first month but the food was actually really good. I was on a 5150 in my teens and the psyc ward in the states...it was the most lonely depressing time of my life. The conditions make a huge difference.
author: @GinaRossana-hl6ph

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Last week I went to the ER and someone was having a some sort of psychiatric break. She was clearly terrified and screaming. Seeing the way staff treated her was heartbreaking. I kept hearing her screaming “get off of me” and hearing the nurses giggling about “the pipes on that chick” and “it’s so and sos favorite patient”. It was very disheartening.
author: @meaganhiller3029

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: It always shocks me how different psych wards can be in different places. I was admitted to a psychiatric institution involuntarily and was there for 2 weeks. I had a roommate, we weren't allowed outside of our room from 9pm-7am and weren't allowed back in our room from 7am-7pm. Eating breakfast, lunch, and dinner was mandatory and we all had to be present in the cafeteria, no one was allowed to stay back. We had to attend group sessions to talk about battling mental illness and coping mechanisms. It wasn't very effective. We had a TV in the common area, but they wouldn't turn it on unless it was to watch a movie. I spent all of my free time reading and coloring. We weren't allowed to go outside and only had one window in the whole ward. It literally felt like prison.
author: @halidyer5595

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This was both hard to watch and incredible to watch.  My extreme admiration goes out to you for documenting something so difficult.  People casually talk of "mental health" as this amorphous concept.  Your bravery in putting yourself out there and attaching a face to a facet of it is extraordinary.  There are so many different struggles people go through.  It's painful seeing someone having to live like that on a daily basis.  I hope you realize just how much of a good you're doing by showing everyone that you can put one foot in front of the other, and that there is no stigma, only a struggle that people have to face down regularly, which takes a lot of strength.  Thank you so much for what you show us all.
author: @revcliff

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: You are so self-aware, Lauren. It’s wonderful that you can understand what’s going on when you’re feeling so troubled right now. You are in my prayers.❤️🙏✨
author: @amynock540

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Sleep is so important for any patient.   We've known that forever. Sadly, hospitals in the U.S.  haven't figured that out.  You are very articulate.  Unfortunately, from what I have heard, there is far from enough activities for psychiatric patients.  Patients sitting around is  typical for psychiatric patients.  .
author: @monaoconnell5650

title: Day in my life as a Sterile Processing Technician #spd #sterileprocessing #surgery #medical
body: I just applied at my local hospital (non-cerified) for this position. Just got an email that my application went to the hiring manager! Wish me luck!

title: Day in my life as a Sterile Processing Technician #spd #sterileprocessing #surgery #medical
body: Wow this is what my mother has been doing for 30+ years. It’s good to actually be able to see more about what she does. Thanks for this video.

title: Day in my life as a Sterile Processing Technician #spd #sterileprocessing #surgery #medical
body: I did sterile processing for nine months, but couldn't continue due to mental and physical health reasons.  It's a hard job, thank you for doing it!

title: Day in my life as a Sterile Processing Technician #spd #sterileprocessing #surgery #medical
body: Thanks for the brief overview of your occupation. I did learn something about the sterilization process in reference to your occupation. Very good career GOD bless you and continue providing good service.❤😊❤😊😂😊❤😊😊❤😊
author: @AmeliaBennett-ph3lq

title: Day in my life as a Sterile Processing Technician #spd #sterileprocessing #surgery #medical
body:
This makes me miss dentistry
As a former dental assistant sterilization was a part of my duties and it was my favorite part of the day because it was less demanding and gave me a little time to decompress from the day. Running things like the ultrasonic and autoclave
Preparing new trays, restocking etc.
I appreciated working on the patients, but working in the healthcare field does take a lot of energy out of you and those moments of decompressing you want to grab at the given moment.
author: @DivinelyBlessed2222

title: A Day in the Life of a Resident
body: .y grandmother thought I should gave become the first Dr in our family. I wish we had the Internet years ago.two of my best friends left pur practice became vets ....but I love peoe do natural medicine is where my first choice would be.
author: @mac-ju5ot

title: A Day in the Life of a Resident
body: If you are interested in medicine or are already in medicine, join our official medical discord here https://discord.gg/CYCvEdN . Called the Medical Knowledge Association, where you learn and ask questions for free! The Live version of the student doctor network.
